# frozen_string_literal: true

class RemoveStatusService < BaseService
  include Redisable
  include Payloadable
  include Lockable

  # Delete a status
  # @param   [Status] status
  # @param   [Hash] options
  # @option  [Boolean] :redraft
  # @option  [Boolean] :immediate
  # @option  [Boolean] :preserve
  # @option  [Boolean] :original_removed
  # @option  [Boolean] :skip_streaming
  def call(status, **options)
    @payload  = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json
    @status   = status
    @account  = status.account
    @options  = options

    with_redis_lock("distribute:#{@status.id}") do
      @status.discard_with_reblogs

      StatusPin.find_by(status: @status)&.destroy

      remove_from_self if @account.local?
      remove_from_followers
      remove_from_lists

      # There is no reason to send out Undo activities when the
      # cause is that the original object has been removed, since
      # original object being removed implicitly removes reblogs
      # of it. The Delete activity of the original is forwarded
      # separately.
      remove_from_remote_reach if @account.local? && !@options[:original_removed]

      # Since reblogs don't mention anyone, don't get reblogged,
      # favourited and don't contain their own media attachments
      # or hashtags, this can be skipped
      unless @status.reblog?
        remove_from_mentions
        remove_reblogs
        remove_from_hashtags
        remove_from_public
        remove_from_media if @status.with_media?
        remove_media
      end

      # Revoke the quote while we get a chance… maybe this should be a `before_destroy` hook?
      RevokeQuoteService.new.call(@status.quote) if @status.quote&.quoted_account&.local? && @status.quote&.accepted?

      @status.destroy! if permanently?
    end
  end

  private

  # The following FeedManager calls all do not result in redis publishes for
  # streaming, as the `:update` option is false

  def remove_from_self
    FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(@account, @status)
  end

  def remove_from_followers
    @account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
      FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, @status)
    end
  end

  def remove_from_lists
    @account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
      FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, @status)
    end
  end

  def remove_from_mentions
    # For limited visibility statuses, the mentions that determine
    # who receives them in their home feed are a subset of followers
    # and therefore the delete is already handled by sending it to all
    # followers. Here we send a delete to actively mentioned accounts
    # that may not follow the account

    return if skip_streaming?

    @status.active_mentions.find_each do |mention|
      redis.publish("timeline:#{mention.account_id}", @payload)
    end
  end

  def remove_from_remote_reach
    # Followers, relays, people who got mentioned in the status,
    # or who reblogged it from someone else might not follow
    # the author and wouldn't normally receive the delete
    # notification - so here, we explicitly send it to them

    status_reach_finder = StatusReachFinder.new(@status, unsafe: true)

    ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(status_reach_finder.inboxes, limit: 1_000) do |inbox_url|
      [signed_activity_json, @account.id, inbox_url]
    end
  end

  def signed_activity_json
    @signed_activity_json ||= serialize_payload(@status, @status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::UndoAnnounceSerializer : ActivityPub::DeleteNoteSerializer, signer: @account, always_sign: true).to_json
  end

  def remove_reblogs
    # We delete reblogs of the status before the original status,
    # because once original status is gone, reblogs will disappear
    # without us being able to do all the fancy stuff

    @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).includes(:account).reorder(nil).find_each do |reblog|
      RemoveStatusService.new.call(reblog, original_removed: true, skip_streaming: skip_streaming?)
    end
  end

  def remove_from_hashtags
    @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: @status.tags.map(&:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
      featured_tag.decrement(@status)
    end

    return unless @status.public_visibility?

    return if skip_streaming?

    @status.tags.map(&:name).each do |hashtag|
      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}", @payload)
      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}:local", @payload) if @status.local?
    end
  end

  def remove_from_public
    return unless @status.public_visibility?

    return if skip_streaming?

    redis.publish('timeline:public', @payload)
    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', @payload)
  end

  def remove_from_media
    return unless @status.public_visibility?

    return if skip_streaming?

    redis.publish('timeline:public:media', @payload)
    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', @payload)
  end

  def remove_media
    return if @options[:redraft]

    if permanently?
      @status.media_attachments.destroy_all
    else
      UpdateMediaAttachmentsPermissionsService.new.call(@status.media_attachments, :private)
    end
  end

  def permanently?
    @options[:immediate] || !(@options[:preserve] || @status.reported?)
  end

  def skip_streaming?
    !!@options[:skip_streaming]
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class BatchedRemoveStatusService < BaseService
  include Redisable

  # Delete multiple statuses and reblogs of them as efficiently as possible
  # @param [Enumerable<Status>] statuses An array of statuses
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Do not modify feeds and send updates to streaming API
  def call(statuses, **options)
    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
      records: statuses,
      associations: options[:skip_side_effects] ? :reblogs : [:account, :tags, reblogs: :account]
    ).call

    statuses_and_reblogs = statuses.flat_map { |status| [status] + status.reblogs }

    # The conversations for direct visibility statuses also need
    # to be manually updated. This part is not efficient but we
    # rely on direct visibility statuses being relatively rare.
    statuses_with_account_conversations = statuses.select(&:direct_visibility?)

    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
      records: statuses_with_account_conversations,
      associations: [mentions: :account]
    ).call

    statuses_with_account_conversations.each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)

    # We do not batch all deletes into one to avoid having a long-running
    # transaction lock the database, but we use the delete method instead
    # of destroy to avoid all callbacks. We rely on foreign keys to
    # cascade the delete faster without loading the associations.
    statuses_and_reblogs.each_slice(50) { |slice| Status.unscoped.where(id: slice.pluck(:id)).delete_all }

    # Since we skipped all callbacks, we also need to manually
    # deindex the statuses
    if Chewy.enabled?
      Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
      Chewy.strategy.current.update(PublicStatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    end

    return if options[:skip_side_effects]

    # Batch by source account
    statuses_and_reblogs.group_by(&:account_id).each_value do |account_statuses|
      account = account_statuses.first.account

      next unless account

      unpush_from_home_timelines(account, account_statuses)
      unpush_from_list_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    end

    # Cannot be batched
    @status_id_cutoff = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(2.weeks.ago)
    redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
      statuses.each do |status|
        unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def unpush_from_home_timelines(account, statuses)
    account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
      statuses.each do |status|
        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, status)
      end
    end
  end

  def unpush_from_list_timelines(account, statuses)
    account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
      statuses.each do |status|
        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, status)
      end
    end
  end

  def unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    return unless status.public_visibility? && status.id > @status_id_cutoff

    payload = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json

    pipeline.publish('timeline:public', payload)
    pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', payload)

    if status.media_attachments.any?
      pipeline.publish('timeline:public:media', payload)
      pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', payload)
    end

    status.tags.map { |tag| tag.name.downcase }.each do |hashtag|
      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}", payload)
      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}:local", payload) if status.local?
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusFilter
  attr_reader :status, :account

  def initialize(status, account)
    @status              = status
    @account             = account
  end

  def filtered?
    return false if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id

    blocked_by_policy? || (account_present? && filtered_status?) || silenced_account?
  end

  def filter_state_for_quote
    if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
      nil
    elsif blocked_by_policy?
      'unauthorized'
    elsif account_present? && blocking_domain?
      'blocked_domain'
    elsif account_present? && blocking_account?
      'blocked_account'
    elsif account_present? && muting_account?
      'muted_account'
    end
  end

  private

  def account_present?
    !account.nil?
  end

  def filtered_status?
    blocking_account? || blocking_domain? || muting_account?
  end

  def blocking_account?
    account.blocking?(status.account_id)
  end

  def blocking_domain?
    account.domain_blocking?(status.account_domain)
  end

  def muting_account?
    account.muting?(status.account_id)
  end

  def silenced_account?
    status_account_silenced? && !account_following_status_account?
  end

  def status_account_silenced?
    status.account.silenced?
  end

  def account_following_status_account?
    account&.following?(status.account_id)
  end

  def blocked_by_policy?
    !policy_allows_show?
  end

  def policy_allows_show?
    StatusPolicy.new(account, status).show?
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusPolicy < ApplicationPolicy
  def show?
    return false if author.unavailable?

    if requires_mention?
      owned? || mention_exists?
    elsif private?
      owned? || following_author? || mention_exists?
    else
      current_account.nil? || (!author_blocking? && !author_blocking_domain?)
    end
  end

  def quote?
    show? && !blocking_author? && record.quote_policy_for_account(current_account) != :denied
  end

  def reblog?
    !requires_mention? && (!private? || owned?) && show? && !blocking_author?
  end

  def favourite?
    show? && !blocking_author?
  end

  def destroy?
    owned?
  end

  alias unreblog? destroy?

  def update?
    owned?
  end

  private

  def requires_mention?
    record.direct_visibility? || record.limited_visibility?
  end

  def owned?
    author.id == current_account&.id
  end

  def private?
    record.private_visibility?
  end

  def mention_exists?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    if record.mentions.loaded?
      record.mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == current_account.id }
    else
      record.mentions.exists?(account: current_account)
    end
  end

  def author_blocking_domain?
    return false if current_account.nil? || current_account.domain.nil?

    author.domain_blocking?(current_account.domain)
  end

  def blocking_author?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.blocking?(author)
  end

  def author_blocking?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.blocked_by?(author)
  end

  def following_author?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.following?(author)
  end

  def author
    record.account
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusReachFinder
  # @param [Status] status
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
  def initialize(status, options = {})
    @status  = status
    @options = options
  end

  def inboxes
    (reached_account_inboxes + followers_inboxes + relay_inboxes).uniq
  end

  private

  def reached_account_inboxes
    scope = Account.where(id: reached_account_ids)
    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
  end

  def reached_account_ids
    # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
    # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one

    if @status.reblog?
      [reblog_of_account_id]
    else
      [
        replied_to_account_id,
        reblog_of_account_id,
        quote_of_account_id,
        mentioned_account_ids,
        reblogs_account_ids,
        quotes_account_ids,
        favourites_account_ids,
        replies_account_ids,
      ].tap do |arr|
        arr.flatten!
        arr.compact!
        arr.uniq!
      end
    end
  end

  def replied_to_account_id
    @status.in_reply_to_account_id if distributable?
  end

  def quote_of_account_id
    @status.quote&.quoted_account_id
  end

  def reblog_of_account_id
    @status.reblog.account_id if @status.reblog?
  end

  def mentioned_account_ids
    @status.mentions.pluck(:account_id)
  end

  # Beware: Quotes can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def quotes_account_ids
    @status.quotes.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Reblogs can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def reblogs_account_ids
    @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Favourites can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def favourites_account_ids
    @status.favourites.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Replies can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def replies_account_ids
    @status.replies.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  def followers_inboxes
    scope = followers_scope
    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
  end

  def relay_inboxes
    if @status.public_visibility?
      Relay.enabled.pluck(:inbox_url)
    else
      []
    end
  end

  def distributable?
    @status.public_visibility? || @status.unlisted_visibility?
  end

  def unsafe?
    @options[:unsafe]
  end

  def followers_scope
    if @status.in_reply_to_local_account? && distributable?
      @status.account.followers.or(@status.thread.account.followers.not_domain_blocked_by_account(@status.account))
    elsif @status.direct_visibility? || @status.limited_visibility?
      Account.none
    else
      @status.account.followers
    end
  end

  def inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
    scope.merge!(Account.without_suspended) unless unsafe?
    scope.inboxes
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class AccountStatusesFilter
  KEYS = %i(
    pinned
    tagged
    only_media
    exclude_replies
    exclude_reblogs
  ).freeze

  attr_reader :params, :account, :current_account

  def initialize(account, current_account, params = {})
    @account         = account
    @current_account = current_account
    @params          = params
  end

  def results
    scope = initial_scope

    scope.merge!(pinned_scope)     if pinned?
    scope.merge!(only_media_scope) if only_media?
    scope.merge!(no_replies_scope) if exclude_replies?
    scope.merge!(no_reblogs_scope) if exclude_reblogs?
    scope.merge!(hashtag_scope)    if tagged?

    scope
  end

  private

  def initial_scope
    return Status.none if account.unavailable?

    if anonymous?
      account.statuses.distributable_visibility
    elsif author?
      exclude_direct? ? account.statuses.where(visibility: %i(public unlisted private)) : account.statuses.all # NOTE: #merge! does not work without the #all
    elsif blocked?
      Status.none
    else
      filtered_scope
    end
  end

  def filtered_scope
    scope = account.statuses

    if exclude_direct?
      scope = scope.where(visibility: follower? ? %i(public unlisted private) : %i(public unlisted))
    else
      scope = account.statuses.left_outer_joins(:mentions)
      scope.merge!(scope.where(visibility: follower? ? %i(public unlisted private) : %i(public unlisted)).or(scope.where(mentions: { account_id: current_account.id })).group(Status.arel_table[:id]))
    end

    scope.merge!(filtered_reblogs_scope) if reblogs_may_occur?

    scope
  end

  def filtered_reblogs_scope
    scope = Status.left_outer_joins(reblog: :account)
    scope
      .where(reblog_of_id: nil)
      .or(
        scope
          # This is basically `Status.not_domain_blocked_by_account(current_account)`
          # and `Status.not_excluded_by_account(current_account)` but on the
          # `reblog` association. Unfortunately, there seem to be no clean way
          # to re-use those scopes in our case.
          .where(reblog: { accounts: { domain: nil } }).or(scope.where.not(reblog: { accounts: { domain: current_account.excluded_from_timeline_domains } }))
          .where.not(reblog: { account_id: current_account.excluded_from_timeline_account_ids })
      )
  end

  def only_media_scope
    Status.without_empty_attachments.joins(:media_attachments).merge(account.media_attachments).group(Status.arel_table[:id])
  end

  def no_replies_scope
    Status.without_replies
  end

  def no_reblogs_scope
    Status.without_reblogs
  end

  def pinned_scope
    account.pinned_statuses.group(Status.arel_table[:id], StatusPin.arel_table[:created_at])
  end

  def hashtag_scope
    tag = Tag.find_normalized(params[:tagged])

    if tag
      Status.tagged_with(tag.id)
    else
      Status.none
    end
  end

  def anonymous?
    current_account.nil?
  end

  def author?
    current_account.id == account.id
  end

  def blocked?
    account.blocking?(current_account) || (current_account.domain.present? && account.domain_blocking?(current_account.domain))
  end

  def follower?
    current_account.following?(account)
  end

  def reblogs_may_occur?
    !exclude_reblogs? && !only_media? && !tagged?
  end

  def pinned?
    truthy_param?(:pinned)
  end

  def only_media?
    truthy_param?(:only_media)
  end

  def exclude_direct?
    truthy_param?(:exclude_direct)
  end

  def exclude_replies?
    truthy_param?(:exclude_replies)
  end

  def exclude_reblogs?
    truthy_param?(:exclude_reblogs)
  end

  def tagged?
    params[:tagged].present?
  end

  def truthy_param?(key)
    ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(params[key])
  end
end

=== Vacuum statuses ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

class Vacuum::StatusesVacuum
  include Redisable

  def initialize(retention_period)
    @retention_period = retention_period
  end

  def perform
    vacuum_statuses! if @retention_period.present?
  end

  private

  def vacuum_statuses!
    statuses_scope.in_batches do |statuses|
      # Side-effects not covered by foreign keys, such
      # as the search index, must be handled first.
      statuses.direct_visibility
        .includes(mentions: :account)
        .find_each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
      if Chewy.enabled?
        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:StatusesIndex')
        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:PublicStatusesIndex')
      end

      # Foreign keys take care of most associated records for us.
      # Media attachments will be orphaned.
      statuses.delete_all
    end
  end

  def statuses_scope
    Status.unscoped.kept
      .joins(:account).merge(Account.remote)
      .where(statuses: { id: ...retention_period_as_id })
  end

  def retention_period_as_id
    Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(@retention_period.ago, with_random: false)
  end

  def remove_from_index(status_ids, index)
    with_redis { |redis| redis.sadd(index, status_ids) }
  end
end

content.text:
I now have everything needed. Here is the complete audit.

---

# Status Contract Audit — Mastodon

## Step 1: Orientation

**Project.** Mastodon — a federated (ActivityPub) microblogging server. Ruby on **Rails 8.1** (`Gemfile:8`), Ruby ≥3.3, Postgres + Redis + Sidekiq, Elasticsearch via Chewy, with a React/TS front-end (`app/javascript/`) and a Node streaming server (`streaming/`).

**Code organization** (`app/`):
- `models/` — ActiveRecord; concerns in `models/concerns/` (shared) and `models/concerns/status/` (Status-specific).
- `services/` — business operations (`PostStatusService`, `RemoveStatusService`, …) + `services/activitypub/` for inbound federation processing.
- `workers/` — Sidekiq async jobs; `workers/activitypub/` (outbound delivery), `workers/scheduler/` (cron/maintenance).
- `lib/` — plain-Ruby logic: `feed_manager.rb`, `status_filter.rb`, `status_reach_finder.rb`, `status_cache_hydrator.rb`, `activitypub/activity/*` (inbound activity handlers).
- `policies/` (authorization), `serializers/` (`rest/` for API, `activitypub/` for federation), `presenters/`, `chewy/` (search indexes), `validators/`.

**The central content model.** `app/models/status.rb` (502 lines) — a `Status` is the thing a user posts. It is the hub everything revolves around: feeds (`FeedManager`), notifications (`Notification` via `activity`), federation (`ActivityPub::*`), moderation (`Report`, `Admin::StatusBatchAction`), search (Chewy indexes). It is built on shared concerns (`Cacheable`, `Discard::Model`, `Paginable`, `RateLimitable`) and Status-specific concerns under `app/models/concerns/status/`.

---

## Step 2: The Status contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb`.

**Shared/global concerns included** (`status.rb:35-46`):
- `Cacheable` (`status.rb:35`) → `concerns/cacheable.rb`: `cache_associated`, `cache_ids` (drives `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS`, `status.rb:48-60,180-184`).
- `Discard::Model` (`status.rb:36`) → gem; soft-delete via `discard_column = :deleted_at` (`status.rb:66`). **This is the core of the teardown contract** — `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) silently hides discarded rows everywhere.
- `Paginable` (`status.rb:37`), `RateLimitable` (`status.rb:38`, `rate_limit by: :account, family: :statuses`, `status.rb:64`).

**Status-specific concerns** (`app/models/concerns/status/`):
- `Visibility` (`visibility.rb`): the `visibility` enum `{public,unlisted,private,direct,limited}` (`visibility.rb:7-10`); scopes `distributable_visibility`, `list_eligible_visibility`, `not_direct_visibility` (`visibility.rb:12-14`); `distributable?`/`hidden?`/`sign?` (`visibility.rb:27-35`); `set_visibility` callback (`visibility.rb:39`).
- `ThreadingConcern` (`threading_concern.rb`): `ancestors`/`descendants`/`self_replies` (`:24,:28,:32`), recursive-CTE tree queries (`:51,:76`), and the class method `permitted_statuses_from_ids` (`:7`) that applies `StatusFilter`.
- `SnapshotConcern` (`snapshot_concern.rb`): `has_many :edits` (`:7`, `dependent: :destroy`), `edited?`, `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!` (`:14,:33`) — edit history.
- `SearchConcern` (`search_concern.rb`): `scope :indexable` (`:7`), `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`/`searchable_properties` (`:10,:26,:35`) — Chewy index source.
- `SafeReblogInsert` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb`): overrides `_insert_record` (`:20`) so a reblog of a discarded status can't be inserted (DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`, `:72`). **Directly coupled to the discard/teardown mechanism.**
- `InteractionPolicyConcern` (`interaction_policy_concern.rb`): `composed_of :quote_interaction_policy` (`:7`), `quote_policy_for_account` (`:20`), downgrade on non-distributable (`:9`).
- `FaspConcern` (`fasp_concern.rb`): `after_commit` create/update/**destroy** hooks announcing lifecycle to FASP providers (`:7-10`).
- `FetchRepliesConcern` (`fetch_replies_concern.rb`): reply-backfill scopes + `should_fetch_replies?` (`:36`).

**Key associations other records hang off** (`status.rb:75-118`) — note the `dependent:` strategy, which **is** the teardown contract:
- `dependent: :destroy`: `favourites` (`:89`), `bookmarks` (`:90`), `reblogs` (`:91`), `mentions` (`:94`), `tagged_objects` (`:97`), `notification` (as `:activity`, `:114`), `poll` (`:116`), `quote` (`:118`), `edits` (snapshot_concern `:7`).
- `dependent: :nullify`: `media_attachments` (`:96`), `quotes` (`:98`).
- `dependent: :delete`: `preview_cards_status` (`:112`).
- `dependent: nil` (orphan-prone / handled elsewhere): `owned_conversation` (`:87`), `replies` (`:93`), `status_stat` (`:115`), `trend` (`:117`).
- Self-refs: `thread` (`in_reply_to_id`, `:83`), `reblog` (`reblog_of_id`, `:84`); `belongs_to :conversation` (`:79`), `:account` (`:77`), `:preloadable_poll` (`:80`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:**
1. `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) — every query excludes discarded statuses unless `unscoped`/`with_discarded`. Changing discard semantics ripples everywhere.
2. Teardown happens **two ways**: `discard_with_reblogs` (soft, `status.rb:400`) and `destroy!` (hard, cascades via `dependent:`). `BatchedRemoveStatusService` bypasses both with `delete_all` + FK cascade (relies on DB FKs, not callbacks).
3. `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` (`status.rb:178,406`) must run before dependent-destroy.
4. Counter caches (`status.rb:160-161,479-493`) and webhooks/FASP fire on commit; `store_uri`/`update_statistics` on create (`:163-164`).
5. `proper` (`:234`, reblog→original indirection) is assumed by nearly every consumer.

---

## Step 3: Creation and distribution path

1. **Entry / persistence (inline):** `PostStatusService#call` → `process_status!` (`post_status_service.rb:84-100`) builds `account.statuses.new`, runs `ProcessMentionsService`, antispam, attaches quote/tagged objects, and `@status.save!` inside a transaction. Snowflake ID assigned via `around_create` (`status.rb:171`).
2. **Post-processing (inline, enqueues async):** `postprocess_status!` (`post_status_service.rb:162-171`): `ProcessHashtagsService` (inline), `Trends.tags.register` (inline), then enqueues `LinkCrawlWorker`, **`DistributionWorker`**, `EmailDistributionWorker`, **`ActivityPub::DistributionWorker`**, `PollExpirationNotifyWorker`, `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker`.
3. **Local fan-out (async):** `DistributionWorker` (`distribution_worker.rb:8`, redis lock `distribute:<id>`) → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12`):
   - inline within the job: `warm_payload_cache!` (`:169`), `deliver_to_self!` (`:71`), notifications via `LocalNotificationWorker` (`:78,:83`), streaming `redis.publish` to public/hashtag timelines (`:146-163`).
   - re-fans to **`FeedInsertWorker`** (async, `:116,:124,:131,:139`) for each follower/list/tag-follower.
4. **Per-recipient feed insert (async):** `FeedInsertWorker#perform` (`feed_insert_worker.rb:7`) → `FeedManager#filter` then `push_to_home`/`push_to_list` (`:61-68`), plus `LocalNotificationWorker` for follow-notify (`:80`).
5. **Federation out (async):** `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` → builds inbox set via `StatusReachFinder` → `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` per inbox.

Inline: validation, save, mentions, hashtags, trends registration, payload-cache warm, streaming publishes, self-home-push. Async: home/list/tag fan-out, notifications, link crawl, email, all ActivityPub delivery, poll-expiry.

## Step 5: How identity & visibility are guarded

**Existence / "is this a real, current status":**
- `Discard::Model` + `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) — discarded rows are invisible to all default queries; teardown code must opt in with `unscoped`/`with_discarded` (`removal_worker.rb:6`, `status_batch_action.rb:14`, `status.rb:370`).
- `DistributionWorker` (`distribution_worker.rb:9-12`) and `FeedInsertWorker` (`feed_insert_worker.rb:24-26`), `RemovalWorker` (`removal_worker.rb:7`), `PushUpdateWorker` etc. all rescue `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` — a deleted status quietly no-ops.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:29-39`) — re-queues if `visibility` is nil (guards against partially-constructed rows).
- `ActivityPub::Activity::Create#delete_arrived_first?` / `delete_later!` (`create.rb:27-29`) under redis lock — guards the create-vs-delete federation race.
- `SafeReblogInsert._insert_record` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb:20,72`) — DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` so you can't reblog a torn-down status.
- `RemoveStatusService` / `StatusReachFinder` use `rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at])` (`remove_status_service.rb:114`, `status_reach_finder.rb:70`) to operate on the in-flight discard set.
- Redis lock `distribute:<id>` shared by `DistributionWorker` and `RemoveStatusService` (`distribution_worker.rb:9`, `remove_status_service.rb:22`) — serializes distribute vs teardown.

**Visibility / "is the viewer allowed to see it":**
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?`/`hidden?` (`visibility.rb:27-35`); scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` (`:12-14`).
- `StatusPolicy#show?` (`status_policy.rb:4-14`) — author availability, mention requirement, private/following, block/domain-block; `reblog?`/`favourite?`/`quote?`/`update?`/`destroy?` build on it (`:16-36`).
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` (`status_filter.rb:11-15`) — wraps `StatusPolicy.show?` plus block/mute/silence; used by `ThreadingConcern.permitted_statuses_from_ids` (`threading_concern.rb:14`).
- `FeedManager#filter_from_home`/`filter_from_mentions?`/`filter_from_tags?`/`filter_from_list?` (`feed_manager.rb:448,486,520,502`) with `build_crutches` (`:625`) — feed-time block/mute/visibility checks.
- `AccountStatusesFilter#initial_scope`/`filtered_scope` (`account_statuses_filter.rb:34-61`) — per-viewer visibility on profile timelines (anonymous→distributable, follower→+private, author→all).
- `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` + `followers_scope` (`status_reach_finder.rb:96-112`) and `inboxes_without_suspended_for` (`:114`) — federation reach gated by visibility and suspension; `unsafe:` bypass for delete fan-out (`:100`, called from `remove_status_service.rb:98`).
- `StatusCacheHydrator#hydrate` (`status_cache_hydrator.rb:8`) — re-applies per-viewer relationship data on the shared cached payload.

## Step 6: Blast radius (ranked by risk)

The change touches three things: **(a) storage/persistence, (b) visibility, (c) teardown of attached data.** Risk ranking:

**🔴 Critical — will break silently if the discard/teardown contract shifts:**
1. `Status::SafeReblogInsert` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb:20,72`) — hand-written SQL keyed on `deleted_at IS NULL`. Any change to the discard column/semantics breaks reblog insertion at the DB layer. **Re-verify first.**
2. `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) — every consumer assumes discarded rows are auto-hidden. Changing discard breaks visibility everywhere at once.
3. `BatchedRemoveStatusService` (`batched_remove_status_service.rb:34-41`) — uses `delete_all` + **FK cascade** (no callbacks) + manual deindex. If `dependent:` strategy or FK structure changes, this orphans records (media, stats) or skips deindex. Used by account deletion.
4. `Vacuum::StatusesVacuum` (`statuses_vacuum.rb`) — same FK-cascade assumption + must call `unlink_from_conversations!` and manual Chewy removal before `delete_all`. Hard-deletes; a contract change here loses data or orphans.
5. `RemoveStatusService` (`remove_status_service.rb:22-54`) — the canonical teardown; `discard_with_reblogs`, reblog removal via `rewhere(deleted_at: …)` (`:114`), conditional `destroy!`/`reported?` preservation (`:162-164`). Central path.
6. `Status#discard_with_reblogs` / `unlink_from_conversations!` / `before_destroy prepend` (`status.rb:400-415,178`) — ordering-sensitive; the `prepend: true` exists specifically so it runs before dependent-destroy.

**🟠 High — depend on visibility or `dependent:` cascade:**
7. The `dependent: :destroy/:nullify/:delete/nil` association set (`status.rb:89-118`) — each dependent model (favourites, bookmarks, reblogs, mentions, poll, quote, notification, preview_cards_status, media_attachments, status_stat, edits, tagged_objects). Changing teardown reassigns who cleans these up.
8. `StatusReachFinder` (`status_reach_finder.rb:70,96-112`) — `rewhere(deleted_at:)` + visibility; a botched delete fan-out means **remote servers never get the Delete** (ghost posts).
9. `FanOutOnWriteService` / `FeedInsertWorker` / `FeedManager` — visibility-keyed fan-out; a visibility-storage change mis-routes posts (e.g. private leaking to public timelines). `broadcastable?`/`distributable_visibility` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:188`, `visibility.rb:12`).
10. `StatusPolicy` + `StatusFilter` + `AccountStatusesFilter` — authorization; a visibility change can over- or under-expose statuses.
11. `account_conversation.rb` (`add_status`/`remove_status`, `:74,:87`) — direct-message conversation integrity, driven by `unlink_from_conversations!`.
12. `DeleteAccountService#purge_statuses!` (`delete_account_service.rb:160-217`) — bulk teardown + counter/feed cleanup + `reported_status_ids` preservation.
13. `AccountStatusesCleanupService` / `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy` (`:16-18`, `:59-71`) — relies on `discard_with_reblogs` then async hard-remove.

**🟡 Medium — derive from status, reindex/cache:**
14. Chewy indexes + indexing scheduler + `SearchConcern` — deindex must stay in sync with teardown (manual in batched/vacuum paths).
15. `StatusCacheHydrator` + `fan-out/<id>` cache (`status_cache_hydrator.rb:12`, `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:170`) — stale cache after edit/delete.
16. `UpdateStatusService` / `ProcessStatusUpdateService` — edits + snapshots; visibility downgrade interacts with `InteractionPolicyConcern` (`:9`).
17. FASP hooks (`fasp_concern.rb:7-10`) — fire on create/update/destroy `after_commit`; teardown-path changes (e.g. `delete_all` bypassing callbacks) silently skip them — note batched/vacuum already bypass.
18. Counter caches (`status.rb:160-161,479-493`) — `statuses_count`/`replies_count`/`reblogs_count` drift if teardown bypasses callbacks (batched path does, and compensates manually in `delete_account_service.rb:192-193`).

**🟢 Lower — representation, mostly recompute:** serializers (`rest/*`, `activitypub/*note*`), presenters, backup/export, annual report.

**Must re-verify after the change:** reblog insertion (SafeReblogInsert), full delete fan-out to remote (StatusReachFinder→DeliveryWorker), account deletion (BatchedRemoveStatusService orphans), vacuum FK cascade + deindex, conversation unlinking on direct-message delete, counter-cache accuracy on every teardown path, Chewy deindex on batched/vacuum paths, FASP destroy announce, private/limited visibility never leaking into public timelines/feeds.

---

I now have everything needed. Here is the complete audit.

---

# Status Contract Audit — Mastodon

## Step 1: Orientation

**Project.** Mastodon — a federated (ActivityPub) microblogging server. Ruby on **Rails 8.1** (`Gemfile:8`), Ruby ≥3.3, Postgres + Redis + Sidekiq, Elasticsearch via Chewy, with a React/TS front-end (`app/javascript/`) and a Node streaming server (`streaming/`).

**Code organization** (`app/`):
- `models/` — ActiveRecord; concerns in `models/concerns/` (shared) and `models/concerns/status/` (Status-specific).
- `services/` — business operations (`PostStatusService`, `RemoveStatusService`, …) + `services/activitypub/` for inbound federation processing.
- `workers/` — Sidekiq async jobs; `workers/activitypub/` (outbound delivery), `workers/scheduler/` (cron/maintenance).
- `lib/` — plain-Ruby logic: `feed_manager.rb`, `status_filter.rb`, `status_reach_finder.rb`, `status_cache_hydrator.rb`, `activitypub/activity/*` (inbound activity handlers).
- `policies/` (authorization), `serializers/` (`rest/` for API, `activitypub/` for federation), `presenters/`, `chewy/` (search indexes), `validators/`.

**The central content model.** `app/models/status.rb` (502 lines) — a `Status` is the thing a user posts. It is the hub everything revolves around: feeds (`FeedManager`), notifications (`Notification` via `activity`), federation (`ActivityPub::*`), moderation (`Report`, `Admin::StatusBatchAction`), search (Chewy indexes). It is built on shared concerns (`Cacheable`, `Discard::Model`, `Paginable`, `RateLimitable`) and Status-specific concerns under `app/models/concerns/status/`.

---

## Step 2: The Status contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb`.

**Shared/global concerns included** (`status.rb:35-46`):
- `Cacheable` (`status.rb:35`) → `concerns/cacheable.rb`: `cache_associated`, `cache_ids` (drives `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS`, `status.rb:48-60,180-184`).
- `Discard::Model` (`status.rb:36`) → gem; soft-delete via `discard_column = :deleted_at` (`status.rb:66`). **This is the core of the teardown contract** — `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) silently hides discarded rows everywhere.
- `Paginable` (`status.rb:37`), `RateLimitable` (`status.rb:38`, `rate_limit by: :account, family: :statuses`, `status.rb:64`).

**Status-specific concerns** (`app/models/concerns/status/`):
- `Visibility` (`visibility.rb`): the `visibility` enum `{public,unlisted,private,direct,limited}` (`visibility.rb:7-10`); scopes `distributable_visibility`, `list_eligible_visibility`, `not_direct_visibility` (`visibility.rb:12-14`); `distributable?`/`hidden?`/`sign?` (`visibility.rb:27-35`); `set_visibility` callback (`visibility.rb:39`).
- `ThreadingConcern` (`threading_concern.rb`): `ancestors`/`descendants`/`self_replies` (`:24,:28,:32`), recursive-CTE tree queries (`:51,:76`), and the class method `permitted_statuses_from_ids` (`:7`) that applies `StatusFilter`.
- `SnapshotConcern` (`snapshot_concern.rb`): `has_many :edits` (`:7`, `dependent: :destroy`), `edited?`, `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!` (`:14,:33`) — edit history.
- `SearchConcern` (`search_concern.rb`): `scope :indexable` (`:7`), `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`/`searchable_properties` (`:10,:26,:35`) — Chewy index source.
- `SafeReblogInsert` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb`): overrides `_insert_record` (`:20`) so a reblog of a discarded status can't be inserted (DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`, `:72`). **Directly coupled to the discard/teardown mechanism.**
- `InteractionPolicyConcern` (`interaction_policy_concern.rb`): `composed_of :quote_interaction_policy` (`:7`), `quote_policy_for_account` (`:20`), downgrade on non-distributable (`:9`).
- `FaspConcern` (`fasp_concern.rb`): `after_commit` create/update/**destroy** hooks announcing lifecycle to FASP providers (`:7-10`).
- `FetchRepliesConcern` (`fetch_replies_concern.rb`): reply-backfill scopes + `should_fetch_replies?` (`:36`).

**Key associations other records hang off** (`status.rb:75-118`) — note the `dependent:` strategy, which **is** the teardown contract:
- `dependent: :destroy`: `favourites` (`:89`), `bookmarks` (`:90`), `reblogs` (`:91`), `mentions` (`:94`), `tagged_objects` (`:97`), `notification` (as `:activity`, `:114`), `poll` (`:116`), `quote` (`:118`), `edits` (snapshot_concern `:7`).
- `dependent: :nullify`: `media_attachments` (`:96`), `quotes` (`:98`).
- `dependent: :delete`: `preview_cards_status` (`:112`).
- `dependent: nil` (orphan-prone / handled elsewhere): `owned_conversation` (`:87`), `replies` (`:93`), `status_stat` (`:115`), `trend` (`:117`).
- Self-refs: `thread` (`in_reply_to_id`, `:83`), `reblog` (`reblog_of_id`, `:84`); `belongs_to :conversation` (`:79`), `:account` (`:77`), `:preloadable_poll` (`:80`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:**
1. `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) — every query excludes discarded statuses unless `unscoped`/`with_discarded`. Changing discard semantics ripples everywhere.
2. Teardown happens **two ways**: `discard_with_reblogs` (soft, `status.rb:400`) and `destroy!` (hard, cascades via `dependent:`). `BatchedRemoveStatusService` bypasses both with `delete_all` + FK cascade (relies on DB FKs, not callbacks).
3. `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` (`status.rb:178,406`) must run before dependent-destroy.
4. Counter caches (`status.rb:160-161,479-493`) and webhooks/FASP fire on commit; `store_uri`/`update_statistics` on create (`:163-164`).
5. `proper` (`:234`, reblog→original indirection) is assumed by nearly every consumer.

---

## Step 3: Creation and distribution path

1. **Entry / persistence (inline):** `PostStatusService#call` → `process_status!` (`post_status_service.rb:84-100`) builds `account.statuses.new`, runs `ProcessMentionsService`, antispam, attaches quote/tagged objects, and `@status.save!` inside a transaction. Snowflake ID assigned via `around_create` (`status.rb:171`).
2. **Post-processing (inline, enqueues async):** `postprocess_status!` (`post_status_service.rb:162-171`): `ProcessHashtagsService` (inline), `Trends.tags.register` (inline), then enqueues `LinkCrawlWorker`, **`DistributionWorker`**, `EmailDistributionWorker`, **`ActivityPub::DistributionWorker`**, `PollExpirationNotifyWorker`, `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker`.
3. **Local fan-out (async):** `DistributionWorker` (`distribution_worker.rb:8`, redis lock `distribute:<id>`) → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12`):
   - inline within the job: `warm_payload_cache!` (`:169`), `deliver_to_self!` (`:71`), notifications via `LocalNotificationWorker` (`:78,:83`), streaming `redis.publish` to public/hashtag timelines (`:146-163`).
   - re-fans to **`FeedInsertWorker`** (async, `:116,:124,:131,:139`) for each follower/list/tag-follower.
4. **Per-recipient feed insert (async):** `FeedInsertWorker#perform` (`feed_insert_worker.rb:7`) → `FeedManager#filter` then `push_to_home`/`push_to_list` (`:61-68`), plus `LocalNotificationWorker` for follow-notify (`:80`).
5. **Federation out (async):** `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` → builds inbox set via `StatusReachFinder` → `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` per inbox.

Inline: validation, save, mentions, hashtags, trends registration, payload-cache warm, streaming publishes, self-home-push. Async: home/list/tag fan-out, notifications, link crawl, email, all ActivityPub delivery, poll-expiry.

---

## Step 4: Every dependent of the Status contract

Grouped by area. Each is an individual dependent.

### A. Feeds & timelines
- `app/lib/feed_manager.rb` — `push_to_home`/`unpush_from_home`/`push_to_list`/`unpush_from_list` (`:76,:90,:102,:117`), `filter`/`filter_from_home`/`filter_from_mentions?`/`filter_from_tags?`/`filter_from_list?` (`:49,:448,:486,:520,:502`), `merge_into_home`/`merge_into_list` (`:128,:156`), `populate_home`/`populate_list` (`:280,:325`), `add_to_feed`/`remove_from_feed` (`:537,:585`), `build_crutches` (`:625`). Uses `list_eligible_visibility`, `reblog`, `proper`.
- `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb` — primary local distributor (see Step 3).
- `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb` — per-recipient insert/unpush/notify.
- `app/services/precompute_feed_service.rb` — regenerates a home feed from statuses.
- `app/workers/regeneration_worker.rb` — wraps `PrecomputeFeedService`.
- `app/services/bootstrap_timeline_service.rb` — seeds new-user timeline.
- `app/lib/account_statuses_filter.rb` — account profile timeline scoping (`:34-101`), visibility-based.
- `app/lib/status_cache_hydrator.rb` — hydrates per-viewer status payload from `fan-out/<id>` cache (`:8,:12,:20`).
- `app/lib/status_finder.rb` — resolves local status from URL.
- `app/presenters/status_relationships_presenter.rb` — batches favourited/reblogged/bookmarked/pinned maps (uses `Status.favourites_map` etc., `status.rb:361-379`).
- `app/workers/push_update_worker.rb` — streaming API update push (`Status.find`).
- `streaming/` (Node) — consumes the `redis.publish` payloads from fan-out/remove.

### B. Federation (ActivityPub)
- `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb` + `app/lib/status_reach_finder.rb` — outbound new-status delivery; reach computed from reblogs/quotes/favourites/replies/mentions/followers (`status_reach_finder.rb:23-112`).

## Step 5: How identity & visibility are guarded

**Existence / "is this a real, current status":**
- `Discard::Model` + `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) — discarded rows are invisible to all default queries; teardown code must opt in with `unscoped`/`with_discarded` (`removal_worker.rb:6`, `status_batch_action.rb:14`, `status.rb:370`).
- `DistributionWorker` (`distribution_worker.rb:9-12`) and `FeedInsertWorker` (`feed_insert_worker.rb:24-26`), `RemovalWorker` (`removal_worker.rb:7`), `PushUpdateWorker` etc. all rescue `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` — a deleted status quietly no-ops.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:29-39`) — re-queues if `visibility` is nil (guards against partially-constructed rows).
- `ActivityPub::Activity::Create#delete_arrived_first?` / `delete_later!` (`create.rb:27-29`) under redis lock — guards the create-vs-delete federation race.
- `SafeReblogInsert._insert_record` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb:20,72`) — DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` so you can't reblog a torn-down status.
- `RemoveStatusService` / `StatusReachFinder` use `rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at])` (`remove_status_service.rb:114`, `status_reach_finder.rb:70`) to operate on the in-flight discard set.
- Redis lock `distribute:<id>` shared by `DistributionWorker` and `RemoveStatusService` (`distribution_worker.rb:9`, `remove_status_service.rb:22`) — serializes distribute vs teardown.

**Visibility / "is the viewer allowed to see it":**
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?`/`hidden?` (`visibility.rb:27-35`); scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` (`:12-14`).
- `StatusPolicy#show?` (`status_policy.rb:4-14`) — author availability, mention requirement, private/following, block/domain-block; `reblog?`/`favourite?`/`quote?`/`update?`/`destroy?` build on it (`:16-36`).
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` (`status_filter.rb:11-15`) — wraps `StatusPolicy.show?` plus block/mute/silence; used by `ThreadingConcern.permitted_statuses_from_ids` (`threading_concern.rb:14`).
- `FeedManager#filter_from_home`/`filter_from_mentions?`/`filter_from_tags?`/`filter_from_list?` (`feed_manager.rb:448,486,520,502`) with `build_crutches` (`:625`) — feed-time block/mute/visibility checks.
- `AccountStatusesFilter#initial_scope`/`filtered_scope` (`account_statuses_filter.rb:34-61`) — per-viewer visibility on profile timelines (anonymous→distributable, follower→+private, author→all).
- `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` + `followers_scope` (`status_reach_finder.rb:96-112`) and `inboxes_without_suspended_for` (`:114`) — federation reach gated by visibility and suspension; `unsafe:` bypass for delete fan-out (`:100`, called from `remove_status_service.rb:98`).
- `StatusCacheHydrator#hydrate` (`status_cache_hydrator.rb:8`) — re-applies per-viewer relationship data on the shared cached payload.

## Step 6: Blast radius (ranked by risk)

The change touches three things: **(a) storage/persistence, (b) visibility, (c) teardown of attached data.** Risk ranking:

**🔴 Critical — will break silently if the discard/teardown contract shifts:**
1. `Status::SafeReblogInsert` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb:20,72`) — hand-written SQL keyed on `deleted_at IS NULL`. Any change to the discard column/semantics breaks reblog insertion at the DB layer. **Re-verify first.**
2. `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) — every consumer assumes discarded rows are auto-hidden. Changing discard breaks visibility everywhere at once.
3. `BatchedRemoveStatusService` (`batched_remove_status_service.rb:34-41`) — uses `delete_all` + **FK cascade** (no callbacks) + manual deindex. If `dependent:` strategy or FK structure changes, this orphans records (media, stats) or skips deindex. Used by account deletion.
4. `Vacuum::StatusesVacuum` (`statuses_vacuum.rb`) — same FK-cascade assumption + must call `unlink_from_conversations!` and manual Chewy removal before `delete_all`. Hard-deletes; a contract change here loses data or orphans.
5. `RemoveStatusService` (`remove_status_service.rb:22-54`) — the canonical teardown; `discard_with_reblogs`, reblog removal via `rewhere(deleted_at: …)` (`:114`), conditional `destroy!`/`reported?` preservation (`:162-164`). Central path.
6. `Status#discard_with_reblogs` / `unlink_from_conversations!` / `before_destroy prepend` (`status.rb:400-415,178`) — ordering-sensitive; the `prepend: true` exists specifically so it runs before dependent-destroy.

**🟠 High — depend on visibility or `dependent:` cascade:**
7. The `dependent: :destroy/:nullify/:delete/nil` association set (`status.rb:89-118`) — each dependent model (favourites, bookmarks, reblogs, mentions, poll, quote, notification, preview_cards_status, media_attachments, status_stat, edits, tagged_objects). Changing teardown reassigns who cleans these up.
8. `StatusReachFinder` (`status_reach_finder.rb:70,96-112`) — `rewhere(deleted_at:)` + visibility; a botched delete fan-out means **remote servers never get the Delete** (ghost posts).
9. `FanOutOnWriteService` / `FeedInsertWorker` / `FeedManager` — visibility-keyed fan-out; a visibility-storage change mis-routes posts (e.g. private leaking to public timelines). `broadcastable?`/`distributable_visibility` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:188`, `visibility.rb:12`).
10. `StatusPolicy` + `StatusFilter` + `AccountStatusesFilter` — authorization; a visibility change can over- or under-expose statuses.
11. `account_conversation.rb` (`add_status`/`remove_status`, `:74,:87`) — direct-message conversation integrity, driven by `unlink_from_conversations!`.
12. `DeleteAccountService#purge_statuses!` (`delete_account_service.rb:160-217`) — bulk teardown + counter/feed cleanup + `reported_status_ids` preservation.
13. `AccountStatusesCleanupService` / `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy` (`:16-18`, `:59-71`) — relies on `discard_with_reblogs` then async hard-remove.

**🟡 Medium — derive from status, reindex/cache:**
14. Chewy indexes + indexing scheduler + `SearchConcern` — deindex must stay in sync with teardown (manual in batched/vacuum paths).
15. `StatusCacheHydrator` + `fan-out/<id>` cache (`status_cache_hydrator.rb:12`, `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:170`) — stale cache after edit/delete.
16. `UpdateStatusService` / `ProcessStatusUpdateService` — edits + snapshots; visibility downgrade interacts with `InteractionPolicyConcern` (`:9`).
17. FASP hooks (`fasp_concern.rb:7-10`) — fire on create/update/destroy `after_commit`; teardown-path changes (e.g. `delete_all` bypassing callbacks) silently skip them — note batched/vacuum already bypass.
18. Counter caches (`status.rb:160-161,479-493`) — `statuses_count`/`replies_count`/`reblogs_count` drift if teardown bypasses callbacks (batched path does, and compensates manually in `delete_account_service.rb:192-193`).

**🟢 Lower — representation, mostly recompute:** serializers (`rest/*`, `activitypub/*note*`), presenters, backup/export, annual report.

**Must re-verify after the change:** reblog insertion (SafeReblogInsert), full delete fan-out to remote (StatusReachFinder→DeliveryWorker), account deletion (BatchedRemoveStatusService orphans), vacuum FK cascade + deindex, conversation unlinking on direct-message delete, counter-cache accuracy on every teardown path, Chewy deindex on batched/vacuum paths, FASP destroy announce, private/limited visibility never leaking into public timelines/feeds.
